{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.
","DIR_RELATIVE":"","LIFECYCLE_SCOPES":"- **`design`** — Design-time activities — specifications, architecture documents, RFCs.
- **`codegen`** — Code generation — schema compilers, scaffolding tools, template processors.
- **`build`** — Build-time activities — compilation, linking, packaging.
- **`test`** — Test-time activities — unit, integration, or acceptance tests.
- **`deploy`** — Deploy-time activities — packaging, provisioning, orchestration.
- **`runtime`** — Runtime activities — the live behaviour of the deployed system.
- **`dev-workflow`** — Developer workflow — tools used during development but not at runtime.

response.rationale: The root directory is a multi-project monorepo containing numerous independent components (agent, connector-lib, frontend, launcher, microprocess, etc.) with distinct purposes, languages, and build systems. This is the workspace root that organizes and coordinates all member components.

entries.inputs:
{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.
","DIR_RELATIVE":"agent-api","LIFECYCLE_SCOPES":"- **`design`** — Design-time activities — specifications, architecture documents, RFCs.
- **`codegen`** — Code generation — schema compilers, scaffolding tools, template processors.
- **`build`** — Build-time activities — compilation, linking, packaging.
- **`test`** — Test-time activities — unit, integration, or acceptance tests.
- **`deploy`** — Deploy-time activities — packaging, provisioning, orchestration.
- **`runtime`** — Runtime activities — the live behaviour of the deployed system.
- **`dev-workflow`** — Developer workflow — tools used during development but not at runtime.
","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"agent-api/Dockerfile":"#See https://aka.ms/customizecontainer to learn how to customize your debug container and how Visual Studio uses this Dockerfile to build your images for faster debugging.

FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:8.0 AS base

WORKDIR /app

EXPOSE 80

FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0 AS build

WORKDIR /app

COPY agent-api/Dull.AgentAPI ./agent-api/Dull.AgentAPI
COPY shared-backend ./shared-backend

RUN dotnet build "./agent-api/Dull.AgentAPI/Dull.AgentAPI/Dull.AgentAPI.csproj" -c Release -o /app/build

FROM build AS publish

WORKDIR /app

RUN dotnet publish "./agent-api/Dull.AgentAPI/Dull.AgentAPI/Dull.AgentAPI.csproj" -c Release -o /app/publish /p:UseAppHost=false

FROM base AS final

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=publish /app/publish .

ENTRYPOINT ["dotnet", "Dull.AgentAPI.dll"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"agent-api/README.md","text":"Agent API"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["agent-api/Dockerfile"],"shebangs":[]}}

response.rationale: This directory contains a Dockerfile that builds a containerized .NET service. The Dockerfile references a dotnet/aspnet base image and publishes a C# project (Dull.AgentAPI.csproj) as the final container entry point, making it a docker-image component that packages a .NET service for deployment.

{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"agent/rust-client/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "rust-client"
version = "2026.3.1"
authors = [
  "Sid Odgers <sid.odgers@cosive.com>",
  "Simon Smart <simon.smart@dull.net>",
  "Aden Herold <aden.herold@dull.net>",
]
# We are currently locked to rust version 2018 as going to 2021 gives us a dependency on GLIBC_2.28
# This is not compatible with the older versions of linux we need to support
edition = "2018"
publish = false

[dependencies]
# MIT or Apache2
aes = "=0.6.0"
agent-api = { workspace = true }
agent-core = { workspace = true }
agent-db = { workspace = true }
agent-tasks = { workspace = true }
# MIT
ansi_term = "=0.12"
# MIT or Apache2
async-trait = "=0.1.57"
# MIT
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }

# for cancellation token:
tokio-util = "0.7"
# for atomic optional statics
atomic-option = "=0.1.2"

# MIT or Apache2
lazy_static = "=1.4.0"
# MIT or Apache2
futures = "0.3"
# MIT or Apache2
log = { version = "=0.4.17", features = ["std"] }
# MIT or Apache2
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde", "std"] }
# MIT or Apache2
structopt = "=0.3.21"
# MIT or Apache2
url = "=2.2.1"
# MIT or Apache2
uuid = { version = "=0.8.2", features = ["v4", "serde"] }
#uuid = { version = "=1.3.1", features = ["v4"] }
# MIT or Apache2
tokio-rustls = "=0.22.0"
# MIT or Apache2
p12 = "=0.2.0"
# Apache-2.0/ISC/MIT
rustls = { version = "=0.19.0", features = ["dangerous_configuration"] }
# MIT or Apache2
base64 = "=0.13.0"
# MIT or Apache2
base64ct = "=1.6.0"
# MIT or Apache2
block-modes = "=0.7.0"
#
bytes = "=1.11.1"
# MIT
cached = "=0.44.0"
# MIT or Apache2
chacha20poly1305 = "=0.7.1"
# MIT or Apache2
crossbeam-queue = "=0.3.1"
# Apache2
crossfire = "=0.1.4"
# MIT or Apache2
ctrlc = { version = "=3.1.8", features = ["termination"] }
env_logger = "0.10.0"
# BSD-2-Clause
git-version = "=0.3.4"
# MIT or Apache2
hex = "=0.4.3"
# MIT or Apache2
hmac = "=0.11.0"
# MIT
http-body-util = "0.1"
# MIT
hyper = { version = "=1.4.1", features = ["client", "http1", "server"] }
# MIT
hyper-util = { version = "0.1", features = ["full"] }
# MIT or Apache2
ipc-channel = { workspace = true }
# MIT or Apache2
local-ip-address = "=0.5.7"
#
num-traits = "=0.2.15"
# MIT
obfstr = "=0.4.4"
# MIT or Apache2
once_cell = "=1.18.0"
# MIT or Apache2
pbkdf2 = { version = "=0.7.5", features = ["sha1", "simple"] }
# MIT or Apache2
pest = "=2.8.0"
# MIT or Apache2
pin-project = "=1.1.0"
# MIT or Apache2
rand = "=0.8.3"
rayon = "=1.10.0"
rayon-core = "=1.12.1"
# MIT or Apache2
rcgen = "=0.8.10"
# MIT or Apache2
regex = "=1.9.6"
# OpenSSL, Original SSLeay and Isc Licenses.
ring = "=0.17.13"
# MIT
rusqlite = { version = "=0.27.0", features = [
  "bundled-sqlcipher-vendored-openssl",
  "hooks",
] }
#
rust_decimal = { version = "=1.29.1", features = ["db-tokio-postgres"] }
# MIT / Apache2 / ISC
rustls-native-certs = "=0.6.2"
# MIT or Apache2
semver = "=0.11.0"
# MIT or Apache2
serde = "=1.0.123"
# MIT
serde-aux = "=2.1.1"
# MIT or Apache2
serde_derive = "=1.0.123"
# MIT or Apache2
serde_json = { version = "=1.0.62", features = ["preserve_order"] }
# MIT or Apache2
serde_qs = "0.13.0"
# MIT or Apache2
serde_repr = "=0.1.6"
# MIT or Apache2
serde_urlencoded = "=0.7.0"
# MIT or Apache2
sha2 = "=0.9.3"
# MIT or Apache2
socket2 = { version = "0.4.7", features = ["all"] }
# MIT or Apache2
stable-eyre = "=0.2.2"
# MIT
sysinfo = "=0.29.11"
# MIT or Apache2
tokio-postgres = { version = "=0.7.0", features = [
  "with-chrono-0_4",
  "with-serde_json-1",
  "with-uuid-0_8",
] }
# uuid = { version = "=1.3.1", features = ["v4"] }
# MIT
tokio-tungstenite = "=0.27.0"
# TODO: Remove once upgraded MSRV
windows-sys = "=0.60.2"

# Libraries only in used for building / testing
[dev-dependencies]
# MIT
dotenv = "=0.15.0"
# MIT or Apache2
# hex = "=0.4.3" # For creating checksum in tests
# BSD-2-Clause
git-version = "=0.3.4"
serial_test = "=0.5.1"
# MIT or Apache2
tokio-postgres = { version = "=0.7.0", features = [
  "with-chrono-0_4",
  "with-uuid-0_8",
] }

[build-dependencies]
# BSD-2-Clause
git-version = "=0.3.4"
# MIT or Apache2
regex = "=1.9.6"
# MIT
walkdir = "=2.3.2"

# Windows only dependencies
[target.'cfg(windows)'.dependencies]
# MIT or Apache2
windows-service = "=0.3.1"
# MIT or Apache2
winapi = { version = "=0.3.9", features = [
    "winsvc",
    "processthreadsapi",
    "winnt",
    "winbase",
    "errhandlingapi",
    "handleapi",
    "securitybaseapi",
    "minwindef",
    "winerror",
    "ntdef",
] }

# Non-FreeBSD dependencies
[target.'cfg(not(target_os = "freebsd"))'.dependencies]
# MIT or Apache2
netstat2 = "=0.9.1"

# Unix only dependencies
[target.'cfg(not(windows))'.dependencies]
# MIT or Apache2
exec = "=0.3.1"
# MIT or Apache2
libc = "=0.2.155"

[features]
shadow_mode = []

[profile.test]
# Speeds up building of tests:
opt-level = 0
debug = false
incremental = true
codegen-units = 8

entries.inputs:
{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

- **`build`** — Build-time activities — compilation, linking, packaging.

- **`deploy`** — Deploy-time activities — packaging, provisioning, orchestration.
- **`runtime`** — Runtime activities — the live behaviour of the deployed system.
- **`dev-workflow`** — Developer workflow — tools used during development but not at runtime.

{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"agent/rust-client/ipc-channel/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "ipc-channel"
version = "0.18.0"
description = "A multiprocess drop-in replacement for Rust channels"
authors = ["The Servo Project Developers"]
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/servo/ipc-channel"
edition = "2021"

[features]
force-inprocess = []
memfd = ["sc"]
unstable = []
async = ["futures", "futures-test"]
win32-trace = []
windows-shared-memory-equality = ["windows/Win32_System_LibraryLoader"]

[dependencies]
bincode = "1"
crossbeam-channel = "0.5"
fnv = "1.0.3"
futures = { version = "0.3", optional = true }
futures-test = { version = "0.3", optional = true }
lazy_static = "1"
libc = "0.2.12"
rand = "0.8"
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["rc"] }
tempfile = "3.4"
uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4"] }

[target.'cfg(any(target_os = "linux", target_os = "openbsd", target_os = "freebsd"))'.dependencies]
mio = { version = "0.8", features = ["os-ext"] }
sc = { version = "0.2.2", optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
crossbeam-utils = "0.8"

[target.'cfg(target_os = "windows")'.dependencies.windows]
version = "0.48.0"
features = [
    "Win32_Foundation",
    "Win32_System_WindowsProgramming",
    "Win32_System_Threading",
    "Win32_System_Pipes",
    "Win32_System_Memory",
    "Win32_System_IO",
    "Win32_Storage_FileSystem",
    "Win32_Security",
]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"agent/rust-client/ipc-channel/README.md","text":"ipc-channel"},{"level":2,"path":"agent/rust-client/ipc-channel/README.md","text":"Overview"},{"level":2,"path":"agent/rust-client/ipc-channel/README.md","text":"Major missing features"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["agent/rust-client/ipc-channel/Cargo.toml"],"shebangs":[{"interpreter":"[feature(test)]","path":"agent/rust-client/ipc-channel/benches/bench.rs"}]}}

response.rationale: This is a Rust library crate providing inter-process communication channels as a multiprocess drop-in replacement for Rust's standard channels. The Cargo.toml declares a named package with dependencies and no bin sections, and the README confirms its library purpose.

entries.inputs:
{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

- **`codegen`** — Code generation — schema compilers, scaffolding tools, template processors.
- **`build`** — Build-time activities — compilation, linking, packaging.
- **`test`** — Test-time activities — unit, integration, or acceptance tests.
- **`deploy`** — Deploy-time activities — packaging, provisioning, orchestration.
- **`runtime`** — Runtime activities — the live behaviour of the deployed system.
- **`dev-workflow`** — Developer workflow — tools used during development but not at runtime.

    COMMENT "Generating dart ffi bindings"

"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib-dart/CHANGELOG.md","text":"1.0.0"},{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib-dart/README.md","text":"Dull Connector wrapper library for Dart"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib-dart/README.md","text":"Features"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib-dart/README.md","text":"Getting started"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib-dart/README.md","text":"Usage"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib-dart/README.md","text":"Rebuilding the generated ffi headers"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib-dart/README.md","text":"Prerequisites:"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib-dart/README.md","text":"Building the c headers and generating dart wrappers:"},{"level":4,"path":"connector-lib-dart/README.md","text":"Manually building the c headers and generating the FFI bindings"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["connector-lib-dart/CMakeLists.txt"],"shebangs":[]}}

response.rationale: A Dart wrapper library for the Dull Connector, built via CMake to generate FFI bindings from C headers. The README and changelog confirm it is a versioned package with documented features and usage patterns.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "base16ct"
version = "0.2.0"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of Base16 a.k.a hexadecimal (RFC 4648) which avoids
any usages of data-dependent branches/LUTs and thereby provides portable
"best effort" constant-time operation and embedded-friendly no_std support
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
documentation = "https://docs.rs/base16ct"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/base16ct"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "encoding", "no-std", "parser-implementations"]
keywords = ["crypto", "hex", "hexadecimal"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.60"

[features]
alloc = []
std = ["alloc"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.2.0 (2023-02-26)"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changed"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.1.1 (2022-01-15)"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Added"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.1.0 (2022-01-12)"},{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/README.md","text":"[RustCrypto]: Constant-Time Base16 (hexadecimal)"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/README.md","text":"Minimum Supported Rust Version"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/README.md","text":"License"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/README.md","text":"Contribution"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["connector-lib/formats/base16ct/Cargo.toml"],"shebangs":[{"interpreter":"[feature(test)]","path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/benches/mod.rs"},{"interpreter":"[no_std]","path":"connector-lib/formats/base16ct/src/lib.rs"}]}}

The presence of `src/lib.rs` (inferred from shebang) and feature flags (alloc, std) confirms it as a pure library with no runnable entry point.

keywords = ["crypto", "base64", "pem", "phc"]
readme = "README.md"

rust-version = "1.65"

[dev-dependencies]
base64 = "0.22"
proptest = { version = "1.2", default-features = false, features = ["std"] }

[features]
alloc = []
std = ["alloc"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]

name = "cmpv2"
version = "0.3.0-pre"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of the Certificate Management Protocol (CMP) as
described in RFC 4210
"""

crmf = "=0.3.0-pre"
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["alloc", "derive", "flagset", "oid"] }
spki = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0" }
x509-cert = { version = "=0.3.0-pre", default-features = false }

hex-literal = "0.4"

- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.

- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/cms/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "cms"
version = "0.3.0-pre"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of the Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS) as described in RFC 5652 and RFC 3274.
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/cms"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "encoding", "no-std", "parser-implementations"]
keywords = ["crypto", "pkcs7", "signing"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.75"

[dependencies]
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["alloc", "derive", "oid", "pem"] }
spki = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0" }
x509-cert = { version = "=0.3.0-pre", default-features = false, features = ["pem"] }
const-oid = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.2", features = ["db"] }

# optional dependencies
aes = { version = "=0.9.0-pre", optional = true }
cbc = { version = "=0.2.0-pre", optional = true }
cipher = { version = "=0.5.0-pre.4", features = ["alloc", "block-padding", "rand_core"], optional = true }
rsa = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.1", optional = true }
sha1 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.3", optional = true }
sha2 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.3", optional = true }
sha3 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.3", optional = true }
signature = { version = "=2.3.0-pre.3", features = ["digest", "alloc"], optional = true }
zeroize = { version = "1.8.1", optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
getrandom = "0.2"
hex-literal = "0.4"
pem-rfc7468 = "=1.0.0-pre.0"
pkcs5 = "=0.8.0-pre.0"
rand = "0.8.5"
rsa = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.1", features = ["sha2"] }
ecdsa = { version = "=0.17.0-pre.5", features = ["digest", "pem"] }
p256 = "=0.14.0-pre.0"

[features]
alloc = ["der/alloc"]
std = ["der/std", "spki/std"]
builder = ["aes", "cbc", "cipher", "rsa", "sha1", "sha2", "sha3", "signature", "std", "spki/alloc", "x509-cert/builder", "zeroize"]
pem = ["alloc", "der/pem"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.2.3 (2024-01-08)"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Added"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.2.2 (2023-07-14)"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Added"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changed"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Fixed"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.2.1 (2023-05-04)"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Added"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.2.0 (2023-03-18)"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.1.0 (2019-05-08)"},{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/README.md","text":"[RustCrypto]: Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS)"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/README.md","text":"About"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/README.md","text":"Minimum Supported Rust Version"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/README.md","text":"License"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/README.md","text":"Contribution"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["connector-lib/formats/cms/Cargo.toml"],"shebangs":[{"interpreter":"[cfg(feature = "builder")]","path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/src/builder.rs"},{"interpreter":"[no_std]","path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/src/lib.rs"},{"interpreter":"[cfg(feature = "builder")]","path":"connector-lib/formats/cms/tests/builder.rs"}]}}

- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/crmf/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "crmf"
version = "0.3.0-pre"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of the Certificate Request Message Format (CRMF) as
described in RFC 4211
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/crmf"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "data-structures", "encoding", "no-std"]
keywords = ["crypto"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.75"

[dependencies]
cms = "=0.3.0-pre"
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["alloc", "derive"] }
spki = "=0.8.0-pre.0"
x509-cert = { version = "=0.3.0-pre", default-features = false }

[dev-dependencies]
const-oid = "=0.10.0-pre.2"

[features]
alloc = ["der/alloc"]
std = ["der/std", "spki/std"]
pem = ["alloc", "der/pem"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/crmf/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/crmf/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.2.0 (2023-06-25)"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/crmf/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.1.0"},{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/crmf/README.md","text":"[RustCrypto]: Certificate Request Message Format (CRMF)"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/crmf/README.md","text":"Minimum Supported Rust Version"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/crmf/README.md","text":"License"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/crmf/README.md","text":"Contribution"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["connector-lib/formats/crmf/Cargo.toml"],"shebangs":[{"interpreter":"[no_std]","path":"connector-lib/formats/crmf/src/lib.rs"}]}}

response.rationale: A Rust crate implementing the Certificate Request Message Format (CRMF) as specified in RFC 4211. The Cargo.toml declares a library package with no binary entry point, and src/lib.rs is present as the library root.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/der/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "der"
version = "0.8.0-pre.0"
description = """
Pure Rust embedded-friendly implementation of the Distinguished Encoding Rules
(DER) for Abstract Syntax Notation One (ASN.1) as described in ITU X.690 with
full support for heapless no_std targets
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/der"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "data-structures", "encoding", "no-std", "parser-implementations"]
keywords = ["asn1", "crypto", "itu", "pkcs"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.71"

[dependencies]
arbitrary = { version = "1.3", features = ["derive"], optional = true }
bytes = { version = "1", optional = true, default-features = false }
const-oid = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.2", optional = true }
der_derive = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", optional = true }
flagset = { version = "0.4.5", optional = true }
pem-rfc7468 = { version = "=1.0.0-pre.0", optional = true, features = ["alloc"] }
time = { version = "0.3.4", optional = true, default-features = false }
zeroize = { version = "1.8", optional = true, default-features = false }

[dev-dependencies]
hex-literal = "0.4.1"
proptest = "1"

[features]
alloc = ["zeroize?/alloc"]
std = ["alloc"]

arbitrary = ["dep:arbitrary", "const-oid?/arbitrary", "std"]
bytes = ["dep:bytes", "alloc"]
derive = ["dep:der_derive"]
oid = ["dep:const-oid"]
pem = ["dep:pem-rfc7468", "alloc", "zeroize"]
real = []

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/der/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/der/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.7.9 entries.inputs:
{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

- **`codegen`** — Code generation — schema compilers, scaffolding tools, template processors.
- **`build`** — Build-time activities — compilation, linking, packaging.
- **`test`** — Test-time activities — unit, integration, or acceptance tests.
- **`deploy`** — Deploy-time activities — packaging, provisioning, orchestration.
- **`runtime`** — Runtime activities — the live behaviour of the deployed system.
- **`dev-workflow`** — Developer workflow — tools used during development but not at runtime.
","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/gss-api/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "gss-api"
version = "0.2.0-pre"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation for Generic Security Service Application Program
Interface (GSS-API)
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/gss-api"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "authentication", "encoding", "no-std", "parser-implementations"]
keywords = ["crypto", "gssapi", "kerberos", "krb5"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.75"

[dependencies]
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["oid", "alloc"] }
spki = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0" }
x509-cert = { version = "=0.3.0-pre", default-features = false }

[dev-dependencies]
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["oid", "pem", "alloc"] }
hex-literal = "0.4"
x509-cert = { version = "=0.3.0-pre", default-features = false, features = ["pem"] }

[features]
rfc2478 = []

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/gss-api/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/gss-api/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.1.0 (2022-05-20)"},{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/gss-api/README.md","text":"[RustCrypto]: GSS-API"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/gss-api/README.md","text":"About"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/gss-api/README.md","text":"Minimum Supported Rust Version"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/gss-api/README.md","text":"License"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/gss-api/README.md","text":"Contribution"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["connector-lib/formats/gss-api/Cargo.toml"],"shebangs":[{"interpreter":"[no_std]","path":"connector-lib/formats/gss-api/src/lib.rs"}]}}

response.rationale: A Rust library crate for GSS-API (Generic Security Service Application Program Interface) recognized by Cargo.toml declaring a package with no [[bin]] section, src/lib.rs as entry point, and library-focused dependencies (der, spki, x509-cert). The crate targets cryptographic and authentication use cases in the RustCrypto ecosystem.
