{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

- **`codegen`** — Code generation — schema compilers, scaffolding tools, template processors.
- **`build`** — Build-time activities — compilation, linking, packaging.
- **`test`** — Test-time activities — unit, integration, or acceptance tests.
- **`deploy`** — Deploy-time activities — packaging, provisioning, orchestration.
- **`runtime`** — Runtime activities — the live behaviour of the deployed system.
- **`dev-workflow`** — Developer workflow — tools used during development but not at runtime.
","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/pkcs1/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "pkcs1"
version = "0.8.0-pre.0"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of Public-Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) #1:
RSA Cryptography Specifications Version 2.2 (RFC 8017)
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/pkcs1"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "data-structures", "encoding", "no-std", "parser-implementations"]
keywords = ["crypto", "key", "pem", "pkcs", "rsa"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.72"

[dependencies]
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["oid"] }
spki = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0" }

# optional dependencies
pkcs8 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.0", optional = true, default-features = false }

[dev-dependencies]
const-oid = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.2", features = ["db"] }
hex-literal = "0.4"
tempfile = "3"

[features]
zeroize = ["der/zeroize"]
alloc = ["der/alloc", "zeroize", "pkcs8?/alloc"]
pem = ["alloc", "der/pem", "pkcs8?/pem"]
std = ["der/std", "alloc"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs1/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs1/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.7.5 response.rationale: This is a Rust library crate implementing PKCS#1 (RSA Cryptography Specifications). The manifest declares dependencies on cryptographic libraries (der, spki, pkcs8) and no binary targets, and the source is marked `no_std`-compatible, indicating a pure library implementation.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "pkcs12"
version = "0.2.0-pre"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of Public-Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) #12:
Personal Information Exchange Syntax v1.1 (RFC7292)
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/pkcs12"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "encoding", "no-std", "parser-implementations"]
keywords = ["crypto", "key", "pkcs", "private"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.75"

[dependencies]
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["alloc", "derive", "oid", "pem"] }
spki = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0" }
x509-cert = { version = "=0.3.0-pre", default-features = false, features = ["pem"] }
const-oid = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.2", features = ["db"] }
cms = "=0.3.0-pre"
digest = { version = "0.11.0-pre.8", features=["alloc"], optional = true }
zeroize = "1.8.1"

[dev-dependencies]
hex-literal = "0.4"
pkcs8 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.0", features = ["pkcs5", "getrandom"] }
pkcs5 = {version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["pbes2", "3des"]}
sha2 = "=0.11.0-pre.3"
whirlpool = "=0.11.0-pre.2"

[features]
kdf = ["dep:digest"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]

"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.1.0 (2024-01-04)"},{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/README.md","text":"[RustCrypto]: PKCS#12 Personal Information Exchange Syntax"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/README.md","text":"Minimum Supported Rust Version"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/README.md","text":"License"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/README.md","text":"Contribution"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/Cargo.toml"],"shebangs":[{"interpreter":"[no_std]","path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/src/lib.rs"},{"interpreter":"[cfg(feature = "kdf")]","path":"connector-lib/formats/pkcs12/tests/kdf.rs"}]}}

response.rationale: This is a Rust library crate implementing PKCS#12 encryption format as part of the RustCrypto project. The Cargo.toml declares dependencies and optional features but no binary targets, and the presence of src/lib.rs confirms a library-focused design.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/pkcs5/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "pkcs5"
version = "0.8.0-pre.0"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of Public-Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) #5:
Password-Based Cryptography Specification Version 2.1 (RFC 8018)
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/pkcs5"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "data-structures", "encoding", "no-std"]
keywords = ["crypto", "key", "pkcs", "password"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.72"

[dependencies]
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["oid"] }
spki = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0" }

# optional dependencies
cbc = { version = "=0.2.0-pre", optional = true }
aes = { version = "=0.9.0-pre", optional = true, default-features = false }
des = { version = "=0.9.0-pre.0", optional = true, default-features = false }
pbkdf2 = { version = "=0.13.0-pre.0", optional = true, default-features = false, features = ["hmac"] }
rand_core = { version = "0.6.4", optional = true, default-features = false }
scrypt = { version = "=0.12.0-pre.0", optional = true, default-features = false }
sha1 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.3", optional = true, default-features = false }
sha2 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.3", optional = true, default-features = false }

[dev-dependencies]
hex-literal = "0.4"

[features]
alloc = []
3des = ["dep:des", "pbes2"]
des-insecure = ["dep:des", "pbes2"]
getrandom = ["rand_core/getrandom"]
pbes2 = ["dep:aes", "dep:cbc", "dep:pbkdf2", "dep:scrypt", "dep:sha2"]
sha1-insecure = ["dep:sha1", "pbes2"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]

- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.

Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/pkcs8/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "pkcs8"
version = "0.11.0-pre.0"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of Public-Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) #8:
Private-Key Information Syntax Specification (RFC 5208), with additional
support for PKCS#8v2 asymmetric key packages (RFC 5958)
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/pkcs8"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "data-structures", "encoding", "no-std", "parser-implementations"]
keywords = ["crypto", "key", "pkcs", "private"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.72"

[dependencies]
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["oid"] }
spki = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0" }

# optional dependencies
rand_core = { version = "0.6", optional = true, default-features = false }
pkcs5 = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", optional = true }
subtle = { version = "2", optional = true, default-features = false }

[dev-dependencies]
hex-literal = "0.4.1"
tempfile = "3"

[features]
alloc = ["der/alloc", "der/zeroize", "spki/alloc"]
std = ["alloc", "der/std", "spki/std"]

3des = ["encryption", "pkcs5/3des"]
des-insecure = ["encryption", "pkcs5/des-insecure"]
encryption = ["alloc", "pkcs5/alloc", "pkcs5/pbes2", "rand_core"]
getrandom = ["rand_core/getrandom"]
pem = ["alloc", "der/pem", "spki/pem"]
sha1-insecure = ["encryption", "pkcs5/sha1-insecure"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]

- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/sec1/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "sec1"
version = "0.8.0-pre.1"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of SEC1: Elliptic Curve Cryptography encoding formats
including ASN.1 DER-serialized private keys as well as the
Elliptic-Curve-Point-to-Octet-String encoding
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/sec1"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "data-structures", "encoding", "no-std", "parser-implementations"]
keywords = ["crypto", "key", "elliptic-curve", "secg"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.72"

[dependencies]
base16ct = { version = "0.2", optional = true, default-features = false }
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", optional = true, features = ["oid"] }
hybrid-array = { version = "0.2.0-rc.8", optional = true, default-features = false }
pkcs8 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.0", optional = true, default-features = false }
serdect = { version = "=0.3.0-pre.0", optional = true, default-features = false, features = ["alloc"] }
subtle = { version = "2", optional = true, default-features = false }
zeroize = { version = "1", optional = true, default-features = false }

[dev-dependencies]
hex-literal = "0.4"
tempfile = "3"

[features]
default = ["der", "point"]
alloc = ["der?/alloc", "pkcs8?/alloc", "zeroize?/alloc"]
std = ["alloc", "der?/std"]

der = ["dep:der", "zeroize"]
pem = ["alloc", "der/pem", "pkcs8/pem"]
point = ["dep:base16ct", "dep:hybrid-array"]
serde = ["dep:serdect"]
zeroize = ["dep:zeroize", "der?/zeroize"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/sec1/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/sec1/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.7.3 ","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/serdect/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "serdect"
description = """
Constant-time serde serializer/deserializer helpers for data that potentially
contains secrets (e.g. cryptographic keys)
"""
version = "0.3.0-pre.0"
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/serdect"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "encoding", "no-std", "parsing"]
keywords = ["crypto", "constant-time", "serde"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.70"

[dependencies]
base16ct = { version = "0.2", default-features = false }
serde = { version = "1.0.184", default-features = false }

# optional features
zeroize = { version = "1", optional = true, default-features = false }

[dev-dependencies]
bincode = "1"
ciborium = "0.2"
hex-literal = "0.4"
proptest = "1"
rmp-serde = "1"
serde = { version = "1.0.184", default-features = false, features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
serde-json-core = { version = "0.5", default-features = false, features = ["std"] }
toml = "0.8"

[features]
default = ["alloc"]
alloc = ["base16ct/alloc", "serde/alloc"]
derive = ["serde/derive"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
targets = []

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/spki/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "spki"
version = "0.8.0-pre.0"
description = """
X.509 Subject Public Key Info (RFC5280) describing public keys as well as their
associated AlgorithmIdentifiers (i.e. OIDs)
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/spki"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "data-structures", "encoding", "no-std"]
keywords = ["crypto", "x509"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.72"

[dependencies]
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["oid"] }

# Optional dependencies
arbitrary = { version = "1.2", features = ["derive"], optional = true }
base64ct = { version = "1", optional = true, default-features = false }
sha2 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.3", optional = true, default-features = false }

[dev-dependencies]
hex-literal = "0.4"
tempfile = "3"

[features]
alloc = ["base64ct?/alloc", "der/alloc"]
std = ["der/std", "alloc"]

arbitrary = ["std", "dep:arbitrary", "der/arbitrary"]
base64 = ["dep:base64ct"]
fingerprint = ["sha2"]
pem = ["alloc", "der/pem"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]

{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.

- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

name = "tai64"
description = "TAI64 and TAI64N (i.e. Temps Atomique International) timestamp support for Rust"
version = "5.0.0-pre"
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/tai64"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
readme = "README.md"
categories = ["date-and-time", "internationalization", "network-programming", "no-std", "parser-implementations"]
keywords = ["tai64", "time", "timestamps"]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.60"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1", optional = true, default-features = false }
zeroize = { version = "1.8", optional = true, default-features = false }

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/tls_codec/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "tls_codec"
version = "0.4.2-pre.1"
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
documentation = "https://docs.rs/tls_codec/"
description = "A pure Rust implementation of the TLS (de)serialization"
readme = "README.md"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/tls_codec"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.60"

[dependencies]
zeroize = { version = "1.8", default-features = false, features = [
    "alloc",
    "zeroize_derive",
] }

# optional dependencies
arbitrary = { version = "1.3", features = ["derive"], optional = true }
tls_codec_derive = { version = "=0.4.1", path = "./derive", optional = true }
serde = { version = "1.0.184", features = ["derive"], optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
clap = "=4.3.23"                                          # pinned to preserve MSRV
clap_lex = "=0.5.0"                                       # pinned to preserve MSRV
anstyle = "=1.0.2"                                        # pinned to preserve MSRV
anstyle-parse = "=0.2.1"                                  # pinned to preserve MSRV
criterion = { version = "0.5", default-features = false }
regex = "1.8"

[features]
default = ["std"]
arbitrary = ["std", "dep:arbitrary"]
derive = ["tls_codec_derive"]
serde = ["std", "dep:serde"]
mls = [] # In MLS variable length vectors are limited compared to QUIC.
std = ["tls_codec_derive?/std"]
conditional_deserialization = [
    "derive",
    "tls_codec_derive/conditional_deserialization",
]

[[bench]]
name = "tls_vec"
harness = false

[[bench]]
name = "quic_vec"
harness = false

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true

{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/x509-cert/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "x509-cert"
version = "0.3.0-pre"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of the X.509 Public Key Infrastructure Certificate
format as described in RFC 5280
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/x509-cert"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "data-structures", "encoding", "no-std"]
keywords = ["crypto"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.75"

[dependencies]
const-oid = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.2", features = ["db"] }
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["alloc", "derive", "flagset", "oid"] }
spki = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["alloc"] }

# optional dependencies
arbitrary = { version = "1.3", features = ["derive"], optional = true }
async-signature = { version = "=0.6.0-pre.1", features = ["digest", "rand_core"], optional = true }
sha1 = { version = "0.11.0-pre.2", optional = true }
signature = { version = "=2.3.0-pre.3", features = ["rand_core"], optional = true }
tls_codec = { version = "0.4.0", default-features = false, features = ["derive"], optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
hex-literal = "0.4"
rand = "0.8.5"
rsa = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.1", features = ["sha2"] }
ecdsa = { version = "=0.17.0-pre.5", features = ["digest", "pem"] }
p256 = "=0.14.0-pre.0"
rstest = "0.19"
sha2 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.3", features = ["oid"] }
tempfile = "3.5.0"
tokio = { version = "1.37.0", features = ["macros", "rt"] }
x509-cert-test-support = { path = "./test-support" }

[features]
default = ["pem", "std"]
std = ["const-oid/std", "der/std", "spki/std", "tls_codec?/std"]

arbitrary = ["dep:arbitrary", "std", "der/arbitrary", "spki/arbitrary"]
builder = ["async-signature", "std", "sha1/default", "signature"]
hazmat = []
pem = ["der/pem", "spki/pem"]
sct = ["dep:tls_codec"]
fingerprint = ["spki/fingerprint"]
base64 = ["spki/base64"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-cert/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-cert/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.2.5 ","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "x509-ocsp"
version = "0.3.0-pre"
description = """
Pure Rust implementation of the X.509 Internet Public Key Infrastructure
Online Certificate Status Protocol - OCSP formats as described in RFC 6960
"""
authors = ["RustCrypto Developers"]
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
homepage = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/tree/master/x509-ocsp"
repository = "https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats"
categories = ["cryptography", "data-structures", "encoding", "no-std"]
keywords = ["crypto", "x509"]
readme = "README.md"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.75"

[dependencies]
const-oid = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.2", default-features = false, features = ["db"] }
der = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["alloc", "derive", "oid"] }
spki = { version = "=0.8.0-pre.0", features = ["alloc"] }
x509-cert = { version = "=0.3.0-pre", default-features = false }

# Optional
digest = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.8", optional = true, default-features = false, features = ["oid"] }
rand_core = { version = "0.6.4", optional = true, default-features = false }
signature = { version = "=2.3.0-pre.3", optional = true, default-features = false, features = ["digest", "rand_core"] }

[dev-dependencies]
hex-literal = "0.4.1"
lazy_static = "1.4.0"
rand = "0.8.5"
rsa = { version = "=0.10.0-pre.1", default-features = false, features = ["sha2"] }
sha1 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.3", default-features = false, features = ["oid"] }
sha2 = { version = "=0.11.0-pre.3", default-features = false, features = ["oid"] }

[features]
rand = ["rand_core"]
builder = ["digest", "rand", "signature"]
std = ["der/std", "x509-cert/std"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Changelog"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.2.1 (2024-01-07)"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/CHANGELOG.md","text":"Added"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.2.0 (2024-01-03) [YANKED]"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/CHANGELOG.md","text":"0.1.0"},{"level":1,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/README.md","text":"[RustCrypto]: X.509 Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/README.md","text":"Minimum Supported Rust Version"},{"level":2,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/README.md","text":"License"},{"level":3,"path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/README.md","text":"Contribution"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/Cargo.toml"],"shebangs":[{"interpreter":"[no_std]","path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/src/lib.rs"},{"interpreter":"[cfg(feature = "builder")]","path":"connector-lib/formats/x509-ocsp/tests/builder.rs"}]}}

- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.

- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.

name = "evm-docker-controller"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
license = "UNLICENSED"
publish = false
description = "Dull - EVM Docker Controller"

[dependencies]
hyper = { version = "1.4.1", features = ["http1", "client", "server"] }
hyper-util = {version = "0.1.3", features = ["tokio"] }
http-body-util = "0.1.1"
tokio = { version = "1.37.0", features = ["full"] }
uuid = { version = "1.8.0", features = ["v4", "serde"] }
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"
serde_urlencoded = "0.7.1"
chrono = "0.4"
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
itertools = "0.14"
bollard = "0.19"
clap = { version = "4.5", features = ["derive"] }
thiserror = "2"
toml = "1.0.7"
privdrop = "0.5.6"
nix = { version = "0.31.2", features = ["user"] }

[package.metadata.deb]
maintainer-scripts = "debian/"

maintainer = "Dull <dull@dull.net>"
assets = [

    ["files/etc/evm-docker-controller/config.toml", "/etc/evm-docker-controller/config.toml", "0600"],

]","evm-docker-controller/Dockerfile":"ARG RUST_VERSION=1.93

CMD ["cargo", "deb"]

entries.inputs:
{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.
","DIR_RELATIVE":"evm-launcher-agent","LIFECYCLE_SCOPES":"- **`design`** — Design-time activities — specifications, architecture documents, RFCs.
- **`codegen`** — Code generation — schema compilers, scaffolding tools, template processors.
- **`build`** — Build-time activities — compilation, linking, packaging.
- **`test`** — Test-time activities — unit, integration, or acceptance tests.
- **`deploy`** — Deploy-time activities — packaging, provisioning, orchestration.
- **`runtime`** — Runtime activities — the live behaviour of the deployed system.
- **`dev-workflow`** — Developer workflow — tools used during development but not at runtime.
","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"evm-launcher-agent/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "evm-agent"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[build]
target = "x86_64-pc-windows-gnu"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
tokio = { version = "=1.20.4", features = ["full"] }
futures = "0.3"
winit = "0.29"
tray-icon = { version = "0.19.1", features = ["common-controls-v6"]}
chrono = "0.4"
windows-sys = { version = "0.59.0", features = ["Win32_UI_Shell"] }
widestring = "1.1.0"
git-version = "=0.3.4"

[build-dependencies]
embed-manifest = "1.4.0"
","evm-launcher-agent/Dockerfile":"FROM buildpack-deps:bookworm

# Install Rust
# from https://github.com/rust-lang/docker-rust/blob/master/stable/bookworm/Dockerfile
ENV RUSTUP_HOME=/usr/local/rustup \
    CARGO_HOME=/usr/local/cargo \
    PATH=/usr/local/cargo/bin:$PATH \
    RUST_VERSION=1.85.0

RUN set -eux; \
    dpkgArch="$(dpkg --print-architecture)"; \
    case "${dpkgArch##*-}" in \
        amd64) rustArch='x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu'; rustupSha256='6aeece6993e902708983b209d04c0d1dbb14ebb405ddb87def578d41f920f56d' ;; \
        armhf) rustArch='armv7-unknown-linux-gnueabihf'; rustupSha256='3c4114923305f1cd3b96ce3454e9e549ad4aa7c07c03aec73d1a785e98388bed' ;; \
        arm64) rustArch='aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu'; rustupSha256='1cffbf51e63e634c746f741de50649bbbcbd9dbe1de363c9ecef64e278dba2b2' ;; \
        i386) rustArch='i686-unknown-linux-gnu'; rustupSha256='0a6bed6e9f21192a51f83977716466895706059afb880500ff1d0e751ada5237' ;; \
        ppc64el) rustArch='powerpc64le-unknown-linux-gnu'; rustupSha256='079430f58ad4da1d1f4f5f2f0bd321422373213246a93b3ddb53dad627f5aa38' ;; \
        s390x) rustArch='s390x-unknown-linux-gnu'; rustupSha256='e7f89da453c8ce5771c28279d1a01d5e83541d420695c74ec81a7ec5d287c51c' ;; \
        *) echo >&2 "unsupported architecture: ${dpkgArch}"; exit 1 ;; \
    esac; \
    url="https://static.rust-lang.org/rustup/archive/1.27.1/${rustArch}/rustup-init"; \
    wget "$url"; \
    echo "${rustupSha256} *rustup-init" | sha256sum -c -; \
    chmod +x rustup-init; \
    ./rustup-init -y --no-modify-path --profile minimal --default-toolchain $RUST_VERSION --default-host ${rustArch}; \
    rm rustup-init; \
    chmod -R a+w $RUSTUP_HOME $CARGO_HOME; \
    rustup --version; \
    cargo --version; \
    rustc --version;

RUN set -eux; \
    apt-get update; \
    apt-get install -y build-essential checkinstall software-properties-common git mingw-w64 clang; 

WORKDIR /app

RUN rustup target add x86_64-pc-windows-gnu

RUN git config --global --add safe.directory '*' 

CMD ["cargo", "build", "--target=x86_64-pc-windows-gnu", "--release"]
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["evm-launcher-agent/Cargo.toml","evm-launcher-agent/Dockerfile"],"shebangs":[{"interpreter":"/bin/bash","path":"evm-launcher-agent/.buildkite/push-to-s3.sh"},{"interpreter":"/bin/bash","path":"evm-launcher-agent/.buildkite/sign-with-kms.sh"},{"interpreter":"[windows_subsystem = "windows"]","path":"evm-launcher-agent/src/main.rs"}]}}

response.rationale: This is a Rust CLI application targeting Windows (x86_64-pc-windows-gnu), evidenced by the Cargo.toml package manifest, the Windows subsystem attribute in main.rs, and the explicit Windows cross-compilation setup. The Dockerfile and build scripts indicate a deployed binary artefact.

{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.
","DIR_RELATIVE":"frontend","LIFECYCLE_SCOPES":"- **`design`** — Design-time activities — specifications, architecture documents, RFCs.
- **`codegen`** — Code generation — schema compilers, scaffolding tools, template processors.
- **`build`** — Build-time activities — compilation, linking, packaging.
- **`test`** — Test-time activities — unit, integration, or acceptance tests.
- **`deploy`** — Deploy-time activities — packaging, provisioning, orchestration.
- **`runtime`** — Runtime activities — the live behaviour of the deployed system.
- **`dev-workflow`** — Developer workflow — tools used during development but not at runtime.
","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"frontend/package.json":"{
  "name": "root",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "build": "turbo build",
    "format": "turbo format",
    "dev": "turbo dev",
    "lint": "turbo lint",
    "test": "turbo test",
    "gen": "turbo gen",
    "build-keycloak-theme": "turbo run build-keycloak-theme --filter=keycloak-theme",
    "start-keycloak": "keycloakify start-keycloak -p apps/keycloak-theme",
    "clean": "find apps packages -type d \\( -name node_modules -o -name dist \\) -prune -exec rm -rf {} + && rm -rf node_modules"
  },
  "packageManager": "pnpm@10.28.2",
  "devDependencies": {
    "@turbo/gen": "^2.7.4",
    "eslint": "^9.39.2",
    "prettier": "^3.8.1",
    "turbo": "^2.7.4",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
  }
}

entries.inputs:
{"COMPONENT_KINDS":"- **`rust-library`** — A Rust crate exposing a library interface. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[lib]` section with no `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/lib.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-cli`** — A Rust crate that produces one or more executable binaries. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` containing a `[[bin]]` section, or by `src/main.rs` alongside a manifest. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`rust-proc-macro`** — A Rust procedural-macro crate. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[lib] proc-macro = true`. Compile-time only; the crate ships no runtime artefact, only a compiler plugin consumed by other crates at build time.
- **`workspace`** — A multi-crate (or multi-package) workspace root. Recognised by `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]`, a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or similar workspace manifests. The root is a component in its own right; its members are child components.
- **`node-library`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package exposing a library interface. Recognised by `package.json` with a `main` or `exports` field and no `bin` field. Whether the source is JS or TS is recorded in the `language` field, not the kind.
- **`node-cli`** — A Node.js / TypeScript package that installs one or more CLI executables. Recognised by `package.json` with a `bin` field.
- **`react-app`** — A React-based frontend application — typically a Create React App, Next.js, Remix, or Vite-based React project. Distinguished from `website` by an explicit React-framework manifest or a top-level `react` / `react-dom` runtime dependency. Use `website` for framework-agnostic frontends.
- **`react-library`** — A library of reusable React components, distributed as an npm package. Recognised by `package.json` declaring `react` as a `peerDependency` plus a library-style entry point.
- **`python-library`** — A Python library package — recognised by `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` without a runnable entry point. Typical lifecycles: build, runtime.
- **`python-app`** — A runnable Python application — typically `pyproject.toml` declaring `[project.scripts]` or `[tool.poetry.scripts]`, or a top-level `__main__.py`. Distinguished from `python-library` by a clear executable entry point.
- **`dart-library`** — A Dart package exposing a library interface. Recognised by a `pubspec.yaml` whose `lib/` tree is the public surface, with no top-level `bin/` directory.
- **`dart-app`** — A non-Flutter Dart application — `pubspec.yaml` plus a `bin/` directory containing the entry point. Use `flutter-app` when the manifest depends on the Flutter SDK.
- **`flutter-app`** — A Flutter application targeting iOS, Android, web, or desktop. Recognised by `pubspec.yaml` declaring `flutter:` as a top-level key and depending on the `flutter` SDK. Distinct from `dart-app` because Flutter has its own build/deploy chain.
- **`dotnet-library`** — A .NET class library — recognised by a `.csproj` / `.fsproj` / `.vbproj` whose `<OutputType>` is `Library` (the default) and which has no `Main` entry point.
- **`dotnet-service`** — A .NET long-running service — recognised by a project file whose `<OutputType>` is `Exe` and which uses a service host (e.g., `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App` framework reference, or a `Program.cs` calling `IHostBuilder`).
- **`website`** — A framework-agnostic static or server-rendered web frontend (e.g., a docs site, a Hugo / Jekyll / Astro project) that does not declare itself as a React app. Use `react-app` for React frontends.
- **`service`** — A long-running server process whose internal language is either unknown or not yet captured by a more specific `<lang>-service` kind — typically a Dockerised opaque binary or a polyglot server.
- **`docker-image`** — A directory rooted at a single `Dockerfile` that builds one container image. The image's contents may themselves be a separate component (e.g., a `node-cli` packaged into the image); this kind describes the packaging artefact.
- **`docker-compose-bundle`** — A directory whose primary artefact is a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yaml`) declaring a multi-image deployment. Used for grouping a set of related services into one component.
- **`installer`** — A directory whose purpose is producing an end-user installer package — e.g., a WiX MSI definition, a `.dmg` build script, a Debian `.deb` packaging tree, or an RPM spec. Distinct from `docker-image` because installers target host installation rather than container runtime.
- **`shell-scripts`** — A directory of standalone shell scripts (bash, zsh, sh) treated as one component. Plural by convention because a single shell file is not itself a component.
- **`sql-scripts`** — A directory of standalone SQL scripts — typically migrations or seed data — treated as one component. Plural by the same convention as `shell-scripts`.
- **`codegen-tool`** — A code-generation tool whose output is consumed by other components — e.g., a schema compiler, a scaffolding generator, or a template processor. The tool's implementation language lives in the `language` field.
- **`config-repo`** — A repository that exists primarily to hold configuration (dotfiles, infrastructure-as-code, declarative deployment specs) rather than code.
- **`docs-repo`** — A repository whose purpose is documentation — prose, reference material, or a specification authored in Markdown or similar.
- **`spec`** — A specification document or collection of documents describing behaviour that other components implement. Distinguished from a docs-repo by its role as a contract rather than reference prose.
- **`external`** — A third-party component referenced from a manifest (e.g., a published crate or npm package). Externals live in `external-components.yaml`, not `components.yaml`.
- **`non-component`** — A directory that looks like a candidate on paper but is confirmed not to be a component — for example, a `.git`-rooted directory whose contents are just metadata, or an empty placeholder.
","DIR_RELATIVE":"frontend/apps/index-page","LIFECYCLE_SCOPES":"- **`design`** — Design-time activities — specifications, architecture documents, RFCs.
- **`codegen`** — Code generation — schema compilers, scaffolding tools, template processors.
- **`build`** — Build-time activities — compilation, linking, packaging.
- **`test`** — Test-time activities — unit, integration, or acceptance tests.
- **`deploy`** — Deploy-time activities — packaging, provisioning, orchestration.
- **`runtime`** — Runtime activities — the live behaviour of the deployed system.
- **`dev-workflow`** — Developer workflow — tools used during development but not at runtime.
","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"frontend/apps/index-page/Dockerfile":"#################################################################
############################# PRUNE #############################
#################################################################

FROM node:22-alpine AS pruner

WORKDIR /app

RUN npm install -g turbo@2.8.7

# Copy the entire frontend monorepo (we'll use turbo to filter in the next step)
# NOTE: this is built from the frontend folder
COPY . .

# Prune the workspace to only include index-page stuff [https://turborepo.dev/docs/reference/prune]
RUN turbo prune index-page --docker

######################################################################
############################## WEB BUILD #############################
######################################################################

FROM node:22-alpine AS web-build

WORKDIR /app

# Copy the lockfile and package.jsons from the pruner stage
# This allows us to install dependencies and cache this layer if deps don't change
COPY --from=pruner /app/out/json/ .
COPY --from=pruner /app/out/pnpm-lock.yaml ./pnpm-lock.yaml

RUN npm install -g turbo@2.8.7
RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy source code from pruner
COPY --from=pruner /app/out/full/ .

RUN pnpm build --filter=index-page


#################################################################
############################# LOCAL #############################
#################################################################

FROM web-build AS local

ENTRYPOINT ["pnpm", "turbo", "--filter", "index-page", "dev"]

#################################################################
############################## PROD #############################
#################################################################

FROM nginx:stable AS production

WORKDIR /app

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV TIMEZONE=Australia/Melbourne
ENV TMPDIR=/tmp
ENV DEBUG=False

RUN apt-get update && apt-get -y install iproute2

# Copy over the build artifacts.
COPY --from=web-build /app/apps/index-page/dist ./dist

# Copy over the configuration files.
# Configure Nginx.
# The Nginx templates are traditionally bind-mounted in a production runtime
# environment (so local config tweaks are possible), but this makes sure the
# "release" versions of the templates are always available in the image.
COPY --from=web-build /app/apps/index-page/scripts/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
COPY --from=web-build /app/apps/index-page/scripts/indexpage.conf.template /etc/nginx/templates/
COPY --from=web-build /app/apps/index-page/scripts/options-ssl-nginx.conf /etc/ssl/
COPY --from=web-build /app/apps/index-page/scripts/ssl-dhparam.pem /etc/ssl/
COPY --from=web-build /app/apps/index-page/scripts/entrypoint.sh /app/scripts/entrypoint.sh

# Set all files under /app/ to be world-readable
# (and world-executable if they were already executable).
RUN chmod -R a+rX /app/

STOPSIGNAL SIGTERM

ENTRYPOINT ["/app/scripts/entrypoint.sh"]

    "name": "index-page",
    "version": "0.0.0",
    "private": true,
    "scripts": {
        "build": "vite build",
        "check-types": "tsc --noEmit",
        "dev": "vite",
        "format": "prettier --write \"src/**/*.{ts,tsx,css}\"",
        "lint": "eslint .",
        "preview": "vite preview"
    },
    "dependencies": {
        "@dull/icons": "workspace:*",
        "@dull/ui": "workspace:*",
        "react": "^18.3.1",
        "react-dom": "^18.3.1",
        "tailwind-variants": "^3.2.2"
    },
    "devDependencies": {
        "@dull/config": "workspace:*",
        "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.1.18",
        "@types/react": "^18.3.24",
        "@types/react-dom": "^18.3.7",
        "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^5.0.2",
        "eslint": "^9.27.0",
        "prettier-plugin-tailwindcss": "^0.6.14",
        "typescript": "~5.8.3",
        "vite": "^7.1.5"
    }
}
"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["frontend/apps/index-page/Dockerfile","frontend/apps/index-page/package.json"],"shebangs":[{"interpreter":"/bin/bash","path":"frontend/apps/index-page/scripts/entrypoint.sh"}]}}

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"frontend/apps/launcher-web/rust/IronRDP/crates/iron-remote-desktop/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "iron-remote-desktop"
version = "0.7.0"
readme = "README.md"
description = "Helper crate for building WASM modules compatible with iron-remote-desktop WebComponent"
edition.workspace = true
license.workspace = true
homepage.workspace = true
repository.workspace = true
authors.workspace = true
keywords.workspace = true
categories.workspace = true

[features]
panic_hook = ["dep:console_error_panic_hook"]

[dependencies]
# WASM
wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = ["HtmlCanvasElement"] }
tracing-web = "0.1"
futures-util = { version = "0.3", features = ["sink", "io"] }

# The `console_error_panic_hook` crate provides better debugging of panics by
# logging them with `console.error`. This is great for development, but requires
# all the `std::fmt` and `std::panicking` infrastructure, so isn't great for
# code size when deploying.
console_error_panic_hook = { version = "0.1", optional = true }

# Logging
tracing = { version = "0.1", features = ["log"] }
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["time"] }

[lints]
workspace = true

response.rationale: A Rust crate with a `Cargo.toml` manifest and `src/lib.rs` entry point that exposes a library interface for building WASM modules. The package metadata and dependencies (wasm-bindgen, web-sys) confirm it is a WebAssembly helper library rather than a standalone binary.

","MANIFEST_CONTENTS":{"frontend/apps/launcher-web/rust/IronRDP/crates/ironrdp-client-glutin/Cargo.toml":"[package]
name = "ironrdp-client-glutin"
version = "0.1.0"
readme = "README.md"
description = "GPU-accelerated RDP client using glutin"
edition.workspace = true
rust-version = "1.89"
license.workspace = true
homepage.workspace = true
repository.workspace = true
authors.workspace = true
keywords.workspace = true
categories.workspace = true

[features]
default = ["rustls"]
rustls = ["ironrdp-tls/rustls"]
native-tls = ["ironrdp-tls/native-tls"]

[dependencies]

# Protocols
ironrdp.workspace = true
ironrdp-tls.workspace = true
sspi = { workspace = true, features = ["network_client"] }

# CLI
clap = { version = "4.2", features = ["derive", "cargo"] }
exitcode = "1.1"

# logging
tracing.workspace = true
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }

# async, futures
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"]}
tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["compat"] }
futures-util = "0.3"

# Utils
chrono = "0.4"
anyhow = "1.0"

# GUI
glutin = "0.29"
ironrdp-glutin-renderer = { path = "../glutin-renderer"}

[lints]
workspace = true

"},"RATIONALE_BUNDLE":{"doc_headings":[{"level":1,"path":"frontend/apps/launcher-web/rust/IronRDP/crates/ironrdp-client-glutin/README.md","text":"GUI client"}],"is_git_root":false,"manifests":["frontend/apps/launcher-web/rust/IronRDP/crates/ironrdp-client-glutin/Cargo.toml"],"shebangs":[]}}

response.rationale: A Rust application that builds an executable GPU-accelerated RDP client using the glutin windowing library. The presence of clap and exitcode dependencies confirms this is a CLI/GUI application rather than a library.
